frameworks.name: reentrant / feedback processing
frameworks.one_line: Top-down PPC feedback recruits V1 for fine-scale category readout.

frameworks.name: multivariate fMRI decoding
frameworks.one_line: Category information read out from voxel patterns across visual and parietal cortex.

frameworks.name: causal influence analysis
frameworks.one_line: Directed inter-area influence quantified to test PPC-to-V1 feedback role.

frameworks.name: perceptual category learning
frameworks.one_line: Flexible categorisation as a plastic cortical process.

core_question: How does feedback from posterior parietal cortex enable flexible, fine-scale visuospatial category representations in early visual cortex?
key_assumptions: Higher-order areas lack fine spatial detail needed for fine discriminations
Category readout can be decoded from BOLD multivoxel patterns
Inter-area causal influence indexes feedback recruitment
manipulations: Trial stage (early / intermediate / late) for time-resolved decoding
Retraining the same stimuli into two new category groups
key_findings: Coarse-category information decoded early in IPS1/2 (PPC)
Fine-category information emerged at intermediate stage in V1 and again late in IPS1/2
Categorisation performance scaled with strength of IPS1/2-to-V1 causal influence
IPS1/2 and V1 selectivity shifted markedly after retraining to new categories
interpretations: Reentrant PPC-visual cortex interactions enable flexible abstraction of category information
Fine-scale categorisation is supported by feedback that reuses early-visual fine spatial detail
